name: setJDay
description: Sets the current date using Julian Day number. Julian Day is the number of days since noon on January 1, 4713 BC. This system is used in astronomy for precise time difference calculations.

parameters.name: JD
parameters.description: Julian Day number (example: 2458849.5 for noon on January 1, 2020)

name: getJDay
description: Returns the current date as a Julian Day number. Used for precise time difference calculations and astronomical computations.

name: setMJDay
description: Sets the current date using Modified Julian Day number. MJD = JD - 2400000.5, which is more convenient for modern calculations.

name: getMJDay
description: Returns the current date as a Modified Julian Day number (MJD = JD - 2400000.5).

name: setDate
signature: setDate(QString dateStr, QString spec, bool dateIsDT)
description:
Sets the simulation date and time using a text string or relative expression. Supports ISO 8601 format and relative expressions like 'now+2days'. This is the most flexible function for time manipulation.
Sets Stellarium's date using an absolute or relative value.
@param dateStr Defines the date or offset to use. Possible formats:
- Date and time according to ISO 8601, without time zone, e.g.,
'2008-03-24T13:21:01'
- Use 'now' to set Stellarium's time to the system's (presumably) real time.
- Specify an offset in the form <sign><value><unit> to set the date relative to its
current value. <unit> may be one of second, minute, hour, day, sol, week, month,
year, with a plural 's' optionally appended, e.g., '+3.25 days', ' - 1 year'.
- Use 'now' followed by an offset to set the date relative to the system's
current value, e.g., 'now+1hour'.
- You may append 'sidereal' to use units derived from the length of a sidereal day,e.g., 'now +2days sidereal'.
@note When sidereal time is used, the length of time for each unit is dependent on
the current planet.  By contrast, when sidereal time is not specified (i.e., solar
time is used) the value is conventional so that 1 day means 1 Earth Solar day.
@param spec May be 'local' or 'utc'. Only has an effect when
an ISO format string is used. Defaults to 'utc'.
 @param dateIsDT set to  true indicates that the given date is formulated in
 Dynamical Time, i.e. with DeltaT added.
@note For fully compatible behaviour of this function with versions 0.11.4
or earlier you should call  core.setDeltaTAlgorithm('WithoutCorrection');
before running  core.setDate(); for disabling DeltaT correction.
 @note Starting with version 0.13.2 all relative dates are set without DeltaT correction.
Starting with version 0.14.0 the Boolean argument has a different meaning and default.

parameters.name: dateStr
parameters.description: Date in ISO 8601 format (e.g., '2008-03-24T13:21:01') or 'now' or 'now+2hours' or '-1day'

parameters.name: dateIsDT
parameters.description: true if the date is in Dynamical Time format (with DeltaT added)

name: getDate
description: Returns the current simulation date and time as an ISO 8601 string. Can return UTC or local time.

name: setTimeRate
description: Sets the simulation time speed. 1 = real time, 2 = twice as fast, 0 = freeze time. Negative values reverse time direction.

name: getTimeRate
description: Returns the current simulation time speed multiplier.

name: isRealTime
description: Checks whether the simulation is in real-time mode (speed = 1 and time synchronized with system).
returnDescription: true if time is real, false if accelerated or frozen

name: setRealTime
description: Sets simulation time to system real time and speed to 1. Used to synchronize simulation with actual time.

name: getDeltaT
description: Returns the DeltaT value (difference between Dynamical Time and Universal Time) for the current date in HMS format. DeltaT is essential for precise astronomical calculations of eclipses.
returnDescription: DeltaT value in HMS format like '0h1m68.2s'

name: getDeltaTsec
description: Returns the DeltaT value in seconds (difference between Dynamical Time and Universal Time).

name: getDeltaTAlgorithm
description: Returns the name of the algorithm used for DeltaT calculation.
example:
var algo = core.getDeltaTAlgorithm();
core.debug("Current DeltaT algorithm: " + algo);

name: setDeltaTAlgorithm
description: Sets the DeltaT calculation algorithm used in the simulation. Different algorithms give different DeltaT values for historical periods.

parameters.name: algorithmName
parameters.description: Algorithm name:  AstronomicalEphemeris, Banjevic, Borkowski, ChaprontMeeus, ChaprontTouze, Clemence, Custom, Espenak, EspenakMeeus, Henriksson2017, IAU, IslamSadiqQureshi, JPLHorizons, MeeusSimons, MontenbruckPfleger, MorrisonStephenson2004, MullerStephenson, Reijs, ReingoldDershowitz, SchmadelZech1979, SchmadelZech1988, Schoch, Stephenson1978, Stephenson1997, StephensonHoulden, StephensonMorrison1984, StephensonMorrison1995, TuckermanGoldstine, WithoutCorrection.

name: isPlanetocentricCalculations
description: Checks whether calculations are performed in planetocentric system (from planet center). On Earth, this means geocentric calculations.
returnDescription: true for planetocentric (geocentric on Earth), false for topocentric

name: setPlanetocentricCalculations
description: Sets the calculation type: planetocentric (from planet center) or topocentric (from observer surface position).

parameters.name: f
parameters.description: true for planetocentric calculations (from planet center), false for topocentric (from observer location)

name: jdFromDateString
description: Converts a date string to a Julian Day number. Used internally by setDate and waitFor.

name: setObserverLocation
signature: setObserverLocation(double longitude, double latitude, double altitude, double duration, QString name, QString planet)
description: Sets the observer location on Earth or other planets with high precision. Can travel between different planets.

parameters.name: altitude
parameters.description: Altitude above sea level in meters (-1000 to ignore)

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: Transition duration to new location in seconds

name: setObserverLocationById
description: Sets the observer location using a location ID from Stellarium's built-in location database.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Location ID (e.g., 'Paris, France' or 'Tokyo, Japan' or 'Greenwich, UnitedKingdom')

name: getObserverLocation
description: Returns the current observer location ID.

name: getObserverLocationInfo
description: Returns detailed information about the current observer location including coordinates, planet, local sidereal time, and more.

name: setTimezone
description: Sets the timezone used for displaying times. Can use IANA timezone names or 'LMST' for local mean solar time.

parameters.name: tz
parameters.description: Timezone name (e.g., 'Africa/Cairo', 'America/New_York') or 'LMST' (Local Mean Solar Time) or 'LTST' (Local True Solar Time)

name: getAllTimezoneNames
description: Returns a list of all available timezone names in the system.

name: geo2utm
signature: QList<double> geo2utm(double longitude, double latitude, int zone)
description: Converts geographic coordinates (longitude, latitude) to UTM (Universal Transverse Mercator) coordinate system.

parameters.name: zone
parameters.description: UTM zone (1-60). If 0, the appropriate zone is calculated automatically

returnDescription: [Easting(m), Northing(m), zone, gamma(deg), k] - UTM coordinates

name: utm2geo
signature: QList<double> utm2geo(double easting, double northing, int zone, bool north)
description: Converts coordinates from UTM (Universal Transverse Mercator) system to geographic coordinates (longitude, latitude).

parameters.name: northing
parameters.description: Northing coordinate in meters (in southern hemisphere, equator = 10000000)

parameters.name: north
parameters.description: true for northern hemisphere, false for southern

name: moveToObject
description: Moves the view center toward a specific celestial object (star, planet, galaxy, comet, asteroid, etc.).

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: English name of the object (e.g., 'Sirius', 'Mars', 'Andromeda Galaxy', 'Halley')

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: Movement duration in seconds (longer duration = slower and smoother movement)

name: moveToSelectedObject
description: Moves the view center toward the currently selected object.

name: moveToAltAzi
signature: moveToAltAzi(QString alt, QString azi, float duration)
description: Moves the view toward specified coordinates in Altitude/Azimuth system. Suitable for telescopes that use this system.

parameters.name: alt
parameters.description: Altitude angle in degrees (0°=horizon, 90°=zenith). Can use '45°', '30d', '0.5rad'

name: moveToRaDec
signature: moveToRaDec(QString ra, QString dec, float duration)
description: Moves the view toward specified coordinates in Right Ascension/Declination system for the current date. This is the equatorial system used in star charts.

name: moveToRaDecJ2000
signature: moveToRaDecJ2000(QString ra, QString dec, float duration)
description: Moves the view toward specified coordinates in Right Ascension/Declination system for the J2000.0 reference frame (epoch of year 2000).

name: moveToGalLongLat
signature: moveToGalLongLat(QString lon, QString lat, float duration)
description: Moves the view toward specified coordinates in Galactic Coordinates system.

name: getViewAltitudeAngle
description: Returns the current altitude angle of the view center. 0=horizon, 90=zenith (overhead), 180=nadir (underfoot), -90=sub-nadir.

name: getViewAzimuthAngle
description: Returns the current azimuth angle of the view center as a compass bearing (0=North, 90=East, 180=South, 270=West).

name: getViewRaAngle
description: Returns the Right Ascension angle of the view center in the current date reference frame.

name: getViewDecAngle
description: Returns the Declination angle of the view center in the current date reference frame.

name: getViewRaJ2000Angle
description: Returns the Right Ascension angle of the view center in the J2000.0 reference frame.

name: getViewDecJ2000Angle
description: Returns the Declination angle of the view center in the J2000.0 reference frame.
example:
var decJ2000 = core.getViewDecJ2000Angle();
core.debug("Dec J2000: " + decJ2000 + "°");

name: setMountMode
description: Sets the mount mode for sky movement. Equatorial makes the sky rotate around the pole, while Alt-Azimuth keeps the sky fixed relative to the horizon.

name: getMountMode
description: Returns the current mount mode ('equatorial' or 'azimuthal').

name: setProjectionMode
description: Sets the map projection type for sky display. Each projection gives a different perspective of the sky.

name: getProjectionMode
description: Returns the current map projection type.
example:
var projection = core.getProjectionMode();
core.debug("Current map projection: " + projection);

name: setDiskViewport
description: Enables or disables circular disk viewport mode (instead of rectangle). Useful for planetarium domes.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: true to enable circular display, false to disable

name: getDiskViewport
description: Returns whether circular disk viewport mode is active.
returnDescription: true if circular display is active, false if rectangular

name: setSphericMirror
description: Enables spherical mirror distortion effect for displaying the sky on spherical screens.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: true to enable spherical mirror distortion

name: setViewportStretch
description: Sets the horizontal stretch factor for the viewport. Experimental, used to adjust display on very wide screens.

compatibility.notes: Experimental function - may change in future versions

name: setFlipHorz
description: Flips the display horizontally (mirror reflection).

name: getFlipHorz
description: Returns the horizontal flip state.

name: setFlipVert
description: Flips the display vertically (mirror reflection).

name: getFlipVert
description: Returns the vertical flip state.

name: setFlagGravityLabels
description: Enables gravity labels - makes text point outward from the screen center.
example:
core.setFlagGravityLabels(true); // Text points outward
core.setFlagGravityLabels(false); // Normal text

name: getFlagGravityLabels
description: Returns the gravity labels state.
returnDescription: true if gravity labels are enabled

name: selectObjectByName
description: Selects a celestial object by its name. Can select stars, planets, deep-sky objects, asteroids, comets, or lunar surface features.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: English name of the object (empty to deselect). For satellites: 'ISS', 'Hubble'

parameters.name: pointer
parameters.description: Show selection pointer (arrow or circle around object)

name: selectConstellationByName
description: Selects a constellation by its name.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: English name of the constellation (e.g., 'Orion', 'Ursa Major', 'Leo')

name: getSelectedObjectInfo
description: Returns detailed information about the currently selected object. Includes coordinates, brightness, distance, size, velocity, and more.
returnDescription: Map containing comprehensive information about the selected object

name: getObjectInfo
description: Returns detailed information about a specific object without needing to select it. Useful for querying multiple objects.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: English name of the object to query

name: addToSelectedObjectInfoString
description: Adds custom text to the selected object's information display. Useful for adding notes or additional data.

parameters.name: str
parameters.description: Text to add to object information

parameters.name: replace
parameters.description: true to replace existing text, false to append

name: getRTS
description: Returns the rise, transit, and set times for a celestial object on the current day. Can specify a different altitude for twilight calculations.

parameters.name: altitude
parameters.description: Required altitude to consider as rise/set in degrees (0 for horizon). Negative values for twilight.

returnDescription: Map containing: rise, transit, set, status-code, status

name: setGuiVisible
description: Shows or hides the entire user interface (toolbars, menus, buttons). Very useful for creating clean presentations or tutorial videos.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: true to show UI, false to hide

name: setSelectedObjectMarkerVisible
description: Shows or hides the arrow or circle marker around the selected object. Makes tracking selected objects easier.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: true to show marker, false to hide

name: setNightMode
description: Enables or disables night mode - changes UI colors to red to preserve night vision during field observations.

parameters.name: b
parameters.description: true to enable night mode, false to disable

name: getNightMode
description: Returns the current night mode state.
returnDescription: true if night mode is enabled

name: setGuiStyle
description: Changes the UI appearance using a custom CSS file. Allows full customization of the program's look.

parameters.name: cssStyle
parameters.description: CSS file name (without .css extension) or 'default' for default settings

name: screenshot
signature: screenshot(QString prefix, bool invert, QString dir, bool overwrite, QString format)
description: Captures a screenshot and saves it as an image file. Supports multiple formats and color inversion.

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: File name prefix (usually gets a sequence number)

parameters.name: overwrite
parameters.description: Overwrite existing files with the same name

name: setMinFps
description: Sets the minimum frames per second (FPS). Affects movement smoothness.

name: getMinFps
description: Returns the current minimum frames per second setting.
example:
var minFps = core.getMinFps();
core.debug("Minimum: " + minFps);

name: setMaxFps
description: Sets the maximum frames per second. Helps save power on mobile devices.

name: getMaxFps
description: Returns the current maximum frames per second setting.
example:
var maxFps = core.getMaxFps();
core.debug("Maximum: " + maxFps);

name: getScreenWidth
description: Returns the width of the main Stellarium window in pixels.

name: getScreenHeight
description: Returns the height of the main Stellarium window in pixels.
example:
var h = core.getScreenHeight();
core.debug("Height: " + h + " pixels");

name: setWindowSize
description: Changes the size of the Stellarium window. Does not exceed actual screen size.

name: goHome
description: Returns to the default home location and view direction.
example:
// Return to default settings
core.goHome();

name: setSkyCulture
description: Changes the sky culture - changes constellations, stars, and artwork according to different cultures around the world.

name: getSkyCulture
description: Returns the current sky culture identifier.
example:
var culture = core.getSkyCulture();
core.debug("Culture ID: " + culture);

name: getSkyCultureName
description: Returns the English name of the current sky culture.
example:
var cultureName = core.getSkyCultureName();
core.debug("English name: " + cultureName);

name: getSkyCultureNameI18n
description: Returns the translated name of the current sky culture based on current UI language.
example:
var cultureName = core.getSkyCultureNameI18n();
core.debug("Translated name: " + cultureName);

name: getAllSkyCultureIDs
description: Returns a list of all available sky culture identifiers in the system.

name: loadSkyImage
signature: loadSkyImage(QString id, QString filename, double lon, double lat, double angSize, double rotation, double minRes, double maxBright, bool visible, QString frame, bool withAberration)
description: Loads an image into the sky (like nebulae or astronomical images) and projects it onto the sky dome with various projection types.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: Image file name (placed in scripts/ folder)

parameters.name: lon
parameters.description: Longitude/Right Ascension of image center (in degrees)

parameters.name: maxBright
parameters.description: Maximum brightness (apparent magnitude per square minute)

name: removeSkyImage
description: Removes a previously loaded sky image and frees memory.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: ID of the image to remove

example:
core.removeSkyImage("myNebula");
core.removeSkyImage("galacticCenter");

name: loadSound
description: Loads an audio file (MP3, WAV, OGG, FLAC) for playback in the script. Must free memory after use.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: Sound file name (placed in scripts/ folder)

name: playSound
description: Plays a previously loaded sound. If stopped, starts from beginning. If paused, resumes.
example: core.playSound("bgMusic");

name: pauseSound
description: Pauses a playing sound. Can be resumed later with playSound.

name: stopSound
description: Stops sound playback completely and rewinds to the beginning.
example: core.stopSound("bgMusic");

name: dropSound
description: Unloads sound from memory and releases resources. Must be called when sound is no longer needed.
example:
core.dropSound("bgMusic");
core.dropSound("explode");

name: getSoundPosition
description: Returns the current playback position of the sound in milliseconds.
returnDescription: Position in milliseconds (0 if stopped, -1 on error)
example:
var pos = core.getSoundPosition("bgMusic");
core.debug("Position: " + (pos/1000).toFixed(1) + " seconds");

name: getSoundDuration
description: Returns the total duration of the sound in milliseconds.
returnDescription: Duration in milliseconds (0 if unknown, -1 on error)
example:
var duration = core.getSoundDuration("bgMusic");
core.debug("Duration: " + (duration/1000).toFixed(1) + " seconds");

name: loadVideo
signature: loadVideo(QString filename, QString id, float x, float y, bool show, float alpha)
description: Loads a video file (MP4, WebM, AVI, MKV) for playback inside the Stellarium window.

name: playVideo
description: Plays a previously loaded video.

parameters.name: keepVisibleAtEnd
parameters.description: Keep video visible after completion (static frame)

example: core.playVideo("vid", true); // Last frame remains after completion

name: playVideoPopout
signature: playVideoPopout(QString id, float fromX, float fromY, float atCenterX, float atCenterY, float finalSizeX, float finalSizeY, float popupDuration, bool frozenInTransition)
description: Plays video with a popout effect - appears from a point, expands gradually, then returns.
example:
// Popout effect from bottom-left corner
core.playVideoPopout("vid", 0, 1, 0.5, 0.5, 0.3, 0.3, 1.5, false);

name: pauseVideo
description: Pauses video playback. Can be resumed later.
example: core.pauseVideo("vid");

name: stopVideo
description: Stops video playback and rewinds to the beginning.
example: core.stopVideo("vid");

name: dropVideo
description: Unloads video from memory and releases resources.
example: core.dropVideo("vid");

name: seekVideo
signature: seekVideo(QString id, qint64 ms, bool pause)
description: Jumps to a specific position in the video.

parameters.name: pause
parameters.description: true to pause after seeking, false to play

example: core.seekVideo("vid", 60000, true); // Jump to 1 minute and pause

name: setVideoXY
signature: setVideoXY(QString id, float x, float y, bool relative)
description: Changes the position of the video frame on screen.

parameters.name: relative
parameters.description: true for relative offset, false for absolute coordinates

example:
core.setVideoXY("vid", 100, 100, false); // Move to (100,100)
core.setVideoXY("vid", 10, 10, true); // Offset by 10 pixels

name: setVideoAlpha
description: Sets the transparency level of the video.

parameters.name: alpha
parameters.description: Transparency level (0 = fully transparent, 1 = fully opaque)

example: core.setVideoAlpha("vid", 0.5); // Half transparent

name: resizeVideo
signature: resizeVideo(QString id, float w, float h)
description: Changes the size of the video frame.

parameters.name: w
parameters.description: New width (if <1, treated as percentage of screen)

example:
core.resizeVideo("vid", 0.5, 0.5); // Half screen size
core.resizeVideo("vid", 640, 480); // Fixed size

name: showVideo
description: Shows or hides the video.

parameters.name: show
parameters.description: true to show, false to hide

example:
core.showVideo("vid", false); // Hide
core.showVideo("vid", true); // Show

name: getVideoDuration
description: Returns the total duration of the video in milliseconds.
example:
var duration = core.getVideoDuration("vid");
core.debug("Video duration: " + (duration/1000).toFixed(1) + " seconds");

name: getVideoPosition
description: Returns the current playback position of the video in milliseconds.
example:
var pos = core.getVideoPosition("vid");
core.debug("Position: " + (pos/1000).toFixed(1) + " seconds");

name: isMediaPlaybackSupported
description: Checks whether media playback is supported in this version of Stellarium.

name: wait
description: Pauses script execution for a specified duration. Useful for creating delays between commands.

name: waitFor
description: Waits until simulation time reaches a specific date/time. Handles time speed and direction.

name: waitForKeypress
description: Pauses script execution until any key is pressed. Useful for creating interactive presentations.

name: setScriptRate
description: Sets the script execution speed. Can accelerate or slow down command execution.

parameters.name: r
parameters.description: Speed multiplier (2 = twice as fast, 0.5 = half speed)

name: getScriptRate
description: Returns the current script execution speed.
example:
var rate = core.getScriptRate();
core.debug("Script speed: " + rate);

name: pauseScript
description: Pauses script execution. Can be resumed from the UI (Ctrl+D, R).

name: exit
description: Stops the current script completely and exits.

name: quitStellarium
description: Closes the Stellarium application completely. Used at the end of automated presentations.

name: debug
description: Prints a debug message to the script console and log file. Useful for script execution tracking.

name: output
description: Writes text to the output.txt file. Useful for storing calculation and analysis results.

name: resetOutput
description: Clears (empties) the output.txt file content.
example:
core.resetOutput();
core.output("Fresh start");

name: saveOutputAs
description: Saves the current output content to a new file. Useful for saving reports without data loss.
example:
core.output("Important data");
core.saveOutputAs("data_" + core.getDate().replace(/:/g, '-') + ".txt");

name: mapToString
description: Converts a QVariantMap to formatted text for printing. Useful for displaying getObjectInfo and similar map contents.

name: clear
description: Resets all display settings to a predefined state. Provides ready-made modes for different presentations.

name: setStelProperty
description: Sets the value of a general property in Stellarium. Covers almost all program settings, including plugin settings.

parameters.name: propertyValue
parameters.description: New value (type compatible with the property)

name: getStelProperty
description: Returns the value of a general property in Stellarium.

name: getPropertyList
description: Returns a list of all available property names in the system.

name: setBortleScaleIndex
description: Sets the Bortle scale index to simulate light pollution. 1 = very dark sky, 9 = heavily polluted urban sky.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: Value from 1 to 9 (1 = darkest sky, 9 = most polluted)

name: getBortleScaleIndex
description: Returns the current Bortle scale index for light pollution simulation.

name: refraction
description: Applies atmospheric refraction correction to altitude angle. Calculates the atmosphere's effect on object positions.

parameters.name: apparent
parameters.description: true: remove refraction (apparent → true), false: add refraction (true → apparent)

name: getScreenXYFromAltAzi
description: Converts Altitude/Azimuth coordinates to screen coordinates (pixels). Useful for placing UI elements over celestial objects.
returnDescription: {x: X coordinate in pixels, y: Y coordinate in pixels}

name: getAppLanguage
description: Returns the current UI language code.

name: setAppLanguage
description: Sets the UI language.
example:
core.setAppLanguage("ar");
core.debug("Language changed to Arabic");

name: translate
description: Translates English text to the current UI language. Useful for creating multilingual messages.

name: isModuleLoaded
description: Checks whether a module (like a plugin) is loaded. Useful for avoiding errors with missing properties.

name: getPlatformName
description: Returns the operating system name where Stellarium is running.

name: getEnv
description: Returns the value of a system environment variable.

name: setSelectedObjectInfo
description: Sets the amount of information displayed about the selected object.
example:
core.setSelectedObjectInfo("AllInfo"); // Show everything
core.setSelectedObjectInfo("ShortInfo"); // Show brief
core.setSelectedObjectInfo("None"); // Hide info

name: say
description: Speaks text using Text-to-Speech engine. Requires Qt 6.6 or later.

name: setDisplayMaxLuminance
description: [Experimental] Sets the maximum display luminance in candela per square meter. Affects tone mapping processing.

parameters.name: cdPerSqM
parameters.description: Luminance value (default: 100), modern screens may reach 300-500

compatibility.notes: Experimental - may change in future versions

name: getDisplayMaxLuminance
description: [Experimental] Returns the current maximum display luminance value.
returnDescription: Luminance in candela per square meter

compatibility.notes: Experimental - may change in future versions

example:
var lum = core.getDisplayMaxLuminance();
core.debug("Max luminance: " + lum);

name: setDisplayAdaptationLuminance
description: [Experimental] Sets the display adaptation luminance to simulate eye adaptation.

compatibility.notes: Experimental - may change in future versions

example: core.setDisplayAdaptationLuminance(30);

name: getDisplayAdaptationLuminance
description: [Experimental] Returns the current display adaptation luminance value.

compatibility.notes: Experimental - may change in future versions

example: var adapt = core.getDisplayAdaptationLuminance();

name: setDisplayGamma
description: [Experimental] Sets the display gamma value. Affects color contrast and sky brightness.

parameters.name: gamma
parameters.description: Gamma value (default: 2.2222 for CRT screens), higher values make sky brighter

compatibility.notes: Experimental - may change in future versions

name: getDisplayGamma
description: [Experimental] Returns the current display gamma value.

compatibility.notes: Experimental - may change in future versions

example: var gamma = core.getDisplayGamma();

name: vec3d
signature: Vec3d vec3d(double x, double y, double z)
description: Creates a 3D vector object of type Double. Used in geometric and astronomical operations.
example:
var direction = core.vec3d(0.5, 0.5, 0.707);
var position = core.vec3d(1, 2, 3);

name: vec3f
signature: Vec3f vec3f(float x, float y, float z)
description: Creates a 3D vector object of type Float.
example: var color = core.vec3f(1.0, 0.0, 0.0); // Red color

name: useQtScript
description: Checks whether the script is running on the old QtScript engine (Qt5).
returnDescription: true if using QtScript, false if using QML

compatibility.notes: Only relevant for Qt5 builds. Returns false on Qt6.
